channel.title: NYT > Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: SpaceX’s Unlikely Journey From Far-Out Idea to $2 Trillion Juggernaut
item.description: Elon Musk said he had initially given SpaceX less than a 10 percent chance of succeeding. His rocket company has come a long way.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac, Kenneth Chang and Kirsten Grind
item.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times
item.media:description: A SpaceX ad on the Nasdaq MarketSite tower in Times Square. The company, which Elon Musk founded in 2002, began trading on the stock market on Friday.

item.title: SpaceX Stock Rises 11% in Largest IPO Ever
item.description: The blockbuster stock market debut of Elon Musk’s rocket company paves the way for a wave of enormous offerings that are coming from OpenAI and Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Traders at the New York Stock Exchange watching news about SpaceX’s initial public offering on the Nasdaq exchange on Friday.

item.title: Elon Musk Becomes World’s First Trillionaire as SpaceX Stock Begins Trading
item.description: With SpaceX shares soaring 20 percent on their first day of trading, the world’s richest person crossed another milestone — one with 13 digits.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Ben Casselman
item.media:credit: Adrees Latif/Reuters
item.media:description: Elon Musk, the chief executive of SpaceX, in 2022 at the company’s facility in Brownsville, Texas.

item.title: What the SpaceX I.P.O. Means for OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: Strong investor interest in SpaceX could be good news for the A.I. rivals, which have signaled that they, too, intend to go public this year.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac

item.title: State Attorneys General Are Investigating OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI said that a coalition of states had opened an investigation over a wide range of its practices, including its handling of user data, safety of minors and advertising activities.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang and David McCabe
item.media:credit: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images
item.media:description: “We take the concerns raised by state attorneys general seriously,” OpenAI, headed by Sam Altman, said in a statement on Saturday.

item.title: U.S. Bars Foreigners From Using Anthropic’s Most Advanced A.I. Models
item.description: The company said on Friday night that the federal government had ordered limits on its Mythos and Fable 5 A.I. systems, citing national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Dustin Volz
item.media:credit: Nicolas Tucat/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Anthropic’s Mythos A.I. model has raised worries that it could be used to attack computer networks.

item.title: If You Have a 401(k), How Much SpaceX Stock Will You Own?
item.description: If you have index funds — such as in a 401(k) — they may soon include shares of the mega company. Here’s a rough calculation.
item.dc:creator: C.J. Robinson
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: ‘Hard Fork’ Live, Part 1: Satya Nadella and Cindy Cohn
item.description: A tech titan, a digital rights defender and two menacing-looking robot dogs took the stage at our second-ever live show.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Rachel Cohn, Whitney Jones, Vjeran Pavic, Alyssa Moxley, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Marion Lozano, Rowan Niemisto and Diane Wong
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times. Photos: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times

item.title: Google Sues to Stop Chinese Cybercrime Group from Using Its A.I.
item.description: In a lawsuit, the tech giant accused the group of using Google’s Gemini system to create hundreds of fake corporate and government websites.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang
item.media:credit: Andria Lo for The New York Times
item.media:description: Google warned that artificial intelligence had supercharged the problem of online scams.

item.title: SpaceX IPO: How Our Reporters Assess the Sky-High Valuation and Potential Economic Impact
item.description: Two Times tech reporters and their editor talk about assessing the sky-high valuation of the rocket and satellite maker, the potential economic impact of its public debut and the role of the world’s richest man in it all.
item.dc:creator: Megan DiTrolio
item.media:credit: Eric Gay/Associated Press
item.media:description: Elon Musk’s rocket maker SpaceX set a price of $135 a share, which would value it at $1.77 trillion.

item.title: SpaceX Finalizes IPO Price at $135 a Share in World’s Largest Public Offering
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket company said it would sell more than 555 million shares at $135 each in its blockbuster initial public offering, which is set to begin trading on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:credit: Mario Tama/Getty Images
item.media:description: SpaceX will begin trading publicly on Friday under the ticker symbol SPCX.

item.title: Skeptics Question Whether SpaceX Is Worth $1.77 Trillion
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket company is spending big and losing money. That has raised questions about whether it can justify its valuation for its blockbuster initial public offering.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: John Raoux/Associated Press
item.media:description: SpaceX has set an initial public offering price of $135 a share, which would value it at $1.77 trillion. Some investors are skeptical that the valuation is justified.

item.title: Jeff Bezos Wants to Build an ‘Artificial General Engineer’
item.description: As co-chief executive of the start-up Prometheus, the Amazon founder is using A.I. to improve how devices ranging from computers to jet engines are made.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Jeenah Moon for The New York Times
item.media:description: With his new company, Prometheus, Jeff Bezos is training A.I. on the design and manufacturing process of various industries.

item.title: How Tesla’s Stock Listing in 2010 Enabled SpaceX’s I.P.O.
item.description: Investors who bought the automaker’s shares in its initial public offering became rich. As a result, many have a deep faith in Elon Musk, the chief executive of Tesla and SpaceX.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.media:credit: Brendan McDermid/Reuters
item.media:description: During Tesla’s initial public offering in 2010, Elon Musk, the company’s chief executive, said, “The smartest money in the world is betting on Tesla.”

item.title: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella Says ‘Everyone Is a Stakeholder’ in A.I.
item.description: At The New York Times’s Hard Fork Live event, Mr. Nadella addressed the backlash against artificial intelligence and President Trump’s comments about Americans sharing in the wealth of A.I. companies
item.dc:creator: Emmy Martin
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Satya Nadella, left, the chief executive of Microsoft, at the Hard Fork Live event in San Francisco on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Muses About Government Taking a Piece of A.I. Companies
item.description: President Trump touched on what is an increasingly hot topic in Washington: how average Americans can get a piece of the tech industry’s A.I. windfall.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle, Ana Swanson and Cecilia Kang
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump, shown here in the Oval Office on Wednesday, has not been shy about the government’s taking a stake in companies.

item.title: Britain Is Weighing a Social Media Ban for Children. How Did It Get Here?
item.description: Months after Australia banned social media for everyone under 16, the British government is considering new policies to keep children safe online.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:credit: Hollie Adams/Reuters
item.media:description: Using a smartphone in Sydney, Australia, last year. In December, the country banned social media for those under 16.

item.title: Antonio Gracias, Elon Musk’s Friend, Is Set to Reap Billions From SpaceX’s IPO
item.description: Antonio Gracias, an investor who has built a $65 billion stake in the company, exemplifies the economic upside of being Mr. Musk’s ally.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Jim Vondruska for The New York Times
item.media:description: Antonio Gracias, left, and Elon Musk at a political event in Green Bay, Wis., last spring. Mr. Gracias and his private equity firm control a $65 billion stake in SpaceX.

item.title: They Tried To Catch a Child Predator on a Livestream. They Trapped Themselves Instead.
item.description: What happened when livestreaming vigilantes ambushed an innocent man.
item.dc:creator: Kashmir Hill and Aric Toler

item.title: SpaceX’s IPO Could Turn 4,400 Employees Into Millionaires
item.description: While Elon Musk may soon become a trillionaire, his rocket company’s market debut is set to change the lives of its current and former employees, too.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grind
item.media:credit: Chelsea Beck

item.title: How to Remove Apps You Never Use (or at Least Hide Them)
item.description: If you need space back on your phone or tablet, removing built-in and unused apps can help free up storage and reduce screen clutter.
item.dc:creator: J. D. Biersdorfer
item.media:credit: Apple
item.media:description: In the Storage settings, Apple’s iOS software for iPad and iPhone shows how much of the device’s storage is being used and offers several ways to reclaim space.

item.title: G.M. Plans to Develop Energy Storage Batteries as E.V. Sales Flag
item.description: The carmaker will follow Tesla in making components for large batteries used by electric utilities, data centers and other businesses to handle fluctuations in power supply and demand.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.media:credit: Rebecca Cook/Reuters
item.media:description: The main active ingredient in the storage batteries that General Motors plans to produce is sodium, which is cheaper and easier to process than lithium.

item.title: Meta A.I. Bug Allowed Hackers to Take Over Instagram Accounts
item.description: The flaw, which Meta said it had fixed, allowed anyone to take over accounts using a bug in the company’s new artificial intelligence software.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac and Eli Tan
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Of the 34,000 accounts, 20,000 were breached, giving hackers access to the account holders’ email addresses, phone numbers, birth dates and other personal data.

item.title: Anthropic Releases ‘Safe’ Version of Its Mythos A.I. Technology
item.description: Called Claude Fable 5, it is twice as expensive as the company’s previous flagship system.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Anthropic’s logo at a company event. The start-up’s Mythos artificial intelligence system caused worries about the security of computer networks.

item.title: Dutch Authorities Block $115 Million Deal by a U.S. Tech Company
item.description: The Netherlands blocked a U.S. company from buying a Dutch firm that handles its national ID system, saying it would create a “threat to the public interest.”
item.dc:creator: Adam Satariano and Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Laurens Van Putten/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Willemijn Aerdts, the Dutch minister for the digital economy and sovereignty, spoke to the news media last month after blocking the acquisition of Solvinity, a Dutch tech company, by the U.S. firm Kyndryl.

item.title: Why Apple’s A.I. Upgrade for Siri Won’t Be Available in Europe
item.description: A regulatory dispute has indefinitely delayed the release of Siri AI.
item.dc:creator: Adam Satariano
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: The keynote at Apple headquarters in Cupertino, Calif., on Monday.

item.title: In the Hybrid A.I.-Human Work Force, Who Will Actually Thrive?
item.description: A panel of experts explains how job seekers should prepare for the future of work.

item.title: OpenAI Files Confidentially for IPO as AI Companies Rush to Wall St.
item.description: The company hopes to raise billions in a highly anticipated public offering that could unlock a new generation of tech industry wealth.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI’s ChatGPT chatbot started the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Apple Reveals New A.I.-Powered Version of Its Siri Digital Assistant
item.description: The iPhone maker revealed its new artificial intelligence products at its developer conference, the last with Tim Cook as chief executive.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Apple held its annual conference for software developers on Monday in Cupertino, Calif.

item.title: Sam Bankman-Fried Has Applied for a Pardon From Trump
item.description: Mr. Bankman-Fried is serving a 25-year prison sentence for fraud related to the collapse of his cryptocurrency exchange, FTX.
item.dc:creator: David McCabe
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
item.media:description: Sam Bankman-Fried, founder of the crypto firm FTX, at Manhattan federal court in 2023.

item.title: How Elon Musk’s Friendship With the F.C.C. Smooths the Way for SpaceX’s I.P.O.
item.description: Brendan Carr, the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission, has greenlighted regulatory requests for the company’s Starlink satellite internet service and lavished praise on its chief executive.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Brendan Carr, chairman of the Federal Communications Commission.

item.title: The Iran War is Forcing Energy-Importing Countries to Turn Inward
item.description: The Iran war is pushing countries to prioritize domestic energy in order to protect themselves from volatile oil and natural gas markets.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jes Aznar for The New York Times
item.media:description: The president of Solaric, a rooftop-solar installer based near Manila, said people were now seeing the technology as “a way to survive this new age.”

item.title: Apple Expected to Detail Its A.I. Plans at Conference
item.description: For the second time, the company is expected to explain its artificial intelligence plans. Unlike some rivals, it is not reorganizing around the technology.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Brian X. Chen

item.title: Two New Studies Ask: Did the iPhone Cause Birthrates to Decline?
item.description: Modern smartphones rolled out in 2007, the year that fertility rates began falling. Two studies say that is not a coincidence.
item.dc:creator: Sabrina Tavernise
item.media:credit: Colin Clark for The New York Times
item.media:description: Fertility is not just dropping in the United States. The mystery is now a near-global phenomenon.

item.title: B.F.I. Preserves ‘Charlie Bit My Finger’ and 430 Videos in Archive of Viral Moments
item.description: The British Film Institute assembled the memes in an archive of culturally significant internet moments. See what made the cut.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
